name: DFAM
description: Drummer From Another Mother. Semi-modular analog percussion voice: 2 VCOs, noise, 24dB Moog ladder filter LP/HP, three decay envelopes and an 8-step analog sequencer with per-step pitch and velocity.

controls.name: VCO 1 FREQUENCY
controls.description: Coarse pitch of oscillator 1.
controls.detail: ~10 octave range · center = reference · summed with VCO 1 CV, VCO EG and sequencer pitch

controls.name: VCO 2 FREQUENCY
controls.description: Coarse pitch of oscillator 2.
controls.detail: ~10 octave range · affected by HARD SYNC and 1→2 FM AMOUNT

controls.name: VCO 1 WAVE
controls.description: Waveform select.
controls.detail: UP: SQUARE · DOWN: TRIANGLE

controls.name: VCO 2 WAVE
controls.description: Waveform select.
controls.detail: UP: SQUARE · DOWN: TRIANGLE

controls.name: VCO 1 EG AMOUNT
controls.description: Depth of VCO envelope on VCO 1 pitch. Bipolar.
controls.detail: Noon = 0 · CW = positive sweep · CCW = negative sweep

controls.name: VCO 2 EG AMOUNT
controls.description: Depth of VCO envelope on VCO 2 pitch. Bipolar.
controls.detail: Noon = 0 · CW/CCW = ± pitch sweep

controls.name: VCO DECAY
controls.description: Decay time of the envelope modulating both VCO pitches.
controls.detail: Audio-rate to several seconds · no effect if both VCO EG AMOUNT knobs at noon

controls.name: SEQ PITCH MOD
controls.description: Three-way switch routes sequencer PITCH 1-8 knobs.
controls.detail: UP: VCO 1 & 2 · CENTER: OFF (PITCH CV still on patchbay) · DOWN: VCO 2 only

controls.name: HARD SYNC
controls.description: Forces VCO 2 phase to reset to VCO 1. Creates metallic, flange-like timbres.
controls.detail: ON: sync engaged · VCO 2 may go silent if its frequency falls below VCO 1, especially on square

controls.name: 1→2 FM AMOUNT
controls.description: Linear FM amount from VCO 1 into VCO 2. Most audible with HARD SYNC OFF.
controls.detail: Summed with 1→2 FM AMT jack · clangorous to bell-like

controls.name: VCO 1 LEVEL
controls.description: Amount of VCO 1 into the filter input.
controls.detail: Square sounds louder than triangle

controls.name: VCO 2 LEVEL
controls.description: Amount of VCO 2 into the filter input.
controls.detail: Square sounds louder than triangle

controls.name: NOISE / EXT LEVEL
controls.description: Amount of white noise (or EXT AUDIO jack signal) into the filter.
controls.detail: Modulated by NOISE LEVEL CV input · EXT AUDIO replaces noise when patched

controls.name: CUTOFF
controls.description: Filter cutoff frequency, LP or HP.
controls.detail: 20Hz – 20kHz · summed with VCF EG, VCF MOD and NOISE/VCF MOD

controls.name: RESONANCE
controls.description: Feedback. Self-oscillates past ~3 o'clock in LP mode.
controls.detail: LP: sine self-oscillation · HP: self-oscillates with CUTOFF ~1kHz, scaling differs

controls.name: VCF
controls.description: Filter mode switch.
controls.detail: UP: HP (24dB/oct) · DOWN: LP (24dB/oct ladder)

controls.name: VCF DECAY
controls.description: Decay time of the envelope modulating filter cutoff.
controls.detail: ~10ms to ~10s · no effect if VCF EG AMOUNT at noon

controls.name: VCF EG AMOUNT
controls.description: Depth of filter envelope. Bipolar.
controls.detail: Noon = 0 · CW = + · CCW = − · overrides VCF switch direction

controls.name: NOISE / VCF MOD
controls.description: Dual-purpose: noise modulates cutoff, OR attenuates external VCF MOD input when patched.
controls.detail: CW adds dirt/lo-fi · patched VCF MOD replaces noise as the source

controls.name: VCA EG
controls.description: Attack-time switch for VCA envelope.
controls.detail: FAST: 1ms (punchy) · SLOW: 100ms (soft)

controls.name: VCA DECAY
controls.description: Decay time of output VCA envelope.
controls.detail: Short = tight hat · long = washed/drone · velocity scales amplitude and decay

controls.name: VOLUME
controls.description: Master output level.
controls.detail: Affects AUDIO OUT and VCA patch jack

controls.name: TEMPO
controls.description: Sequencer clock speed. Continuously variable across a very wide range.
controls.detail: ~0.7Hz – 700Hz per step (≈10 BPM – 10,000 BPM at 16ths) · overridden by ADV/CLOCK when patched

controls.name: PITCH 1-8
controls.description: Per-step pitch knobs. Bipolar around the VCO FREQUENCY reference.
controls.detail: Roughly ±5V (~10 octave range) · routed per SEQ PITCH MOD switch · always present at PITCH output

controls.name: VELOCITY 1-8
controls.description: Per-step velocity. Drives all three envelopes' amplitude and slightly their decay time.
controls.detail: 0–5V · VELOCITY knob at 0 = silent step · normalized to VELOCITY jack

controls.name: RUN/STOP
controls.description: Starts/stops sequencer from current step.
controls.detail: Red LED lit when running

controls.name: ADVANCE
controls.description: Manually steps the sequencer without triggering the envelopes.
controls.detail: Useful for fine-tuning individual steps while stopped

controls.name: TRIGGER
controls.description: Fires all three envelopes at current step without advancing.
controls.detail: Lets a step be auditioned and adjusted repeatedly

inputs.name: TRIGGER
inputs.description: Fires all three EGs at current step velocity without advancing the sequencer.

inputs.name: VCA CV
inputs.description: Direct CV into output VCA. Sums with internal VCA EG.

inputs.name: VELOCITY
inputs.description: Replaces sequencer velocity — drives all three EG amplitudes.

inputs.name: VCA DECAY
inputs.description: CV sums with VCA DECAY knob.
inputs.voltage: −5V to +5V · set knob to noon for widest response

inputs.name: EXT AUDIO
inputs.description: External audio source replaces noise at the mixer.

inputs.name: VCF DECAY
inputs.description: CV sums with VCF DECAY knob.

inputs.name: NOISE LEVEL
inputs.description: CV modulates NOISE/EXT LEVEL knob position.
inputs.voltage: 0 to +8V · can clip the noise VCA

inputs.name: VCO DECAY
inputs.description: CV sums with VCO DECAY knob.

inputs.name: VCF MOD
inputs.description: Hard-wired CV modulation source for VCF cutoff. Replaces noise in the NOISE/VCF MOD circuit.

inputs.name: VCO 1 CV
inputs.voltage: −5V to +5V · set FREQ and EG AMOUNT to noon for accurate tracking

inputs.name: VCO 2 CV
inputs.voltage: −5V to +5V · set FREQ and EG AMOUNT to noon for accurate tracking

inputs.name: TEMPO
inputs.description: 1V/oct input to the internal clock VCO. Extends tempo into audio rate.

inputs.name: ADV / CLOCK
inputs.description: External clock advance. TEMPO knob is ignored while patched. Each rising edge advances one step.
inputs.voltage: 0 to +5V · +10V tolerant · slow-changing CVs may be missed

outputs.name: VCA EG
outputs.description: Copy of internal VCA envelope. Informed by velocity, VCA DECAY, VCA EG switch.

outputs.name: VCF EG
outputs.description: Copy of internal VCF envelope. Informed by velocity and VCF DECAY.

outputs.name: VCO EG
outputs.description: Copy of internal VCO envelope. Informed by velocity and VCO DECAY.

outputs.name: TRIGGER
outputs.description: ~1ms pulse from sequencer clock. Per step, used to clock another DFAM or a drum voice.

outputs.name: VELOCITY
outputs.description: Current step velocity as CV. Follows whichever step the sequencer is on.

outputs.name: PITCH
outputs.description: Current step pitch knob voltage. Always available even when SEQ PITCH MOD is OFF.

behaviors.name: Semi-modular normalization
behaviors.description: Default signal path: two VCOs and noise → mixer → VCF → VCA → OUT. Sequencer clock is normalized to TRIGGER input (fires all three EGs); VELOCITY 1-8 normalized to VELOCITY input; noise normalized to the mixer. Any patched cable replaces its normalization.

behaviors.name: Three velocity-scaled envelopes
behaviors.description: VCO, VCF and VCA each have their own decay envelope. All three share one velocity input — per-step VELOCITY knobs set amplitude of all three simultaneously, and also subtly affect their decay time. Velocity=0 silences the step entirely.

behaviors.name: Bipolar EG amounts
behaviors.description: EG AMOUNT knobs are bipolar around noon. CW produces standard rising envelopes (pitch jumps up, filter opens); CCW produces inverted envelopes (pitch dips, filter closes into each note). Negative pitch gives videogame-style bloops; negative VCF gives inverted drums.

behaviors.name: SEQ PITCH MOD routing
behaviors.description: VCO 1 & 2: sequencer pitch controls both oscillators (sequenced bass lines). VCO 2: only oscillator 2 — useful with HARD SYNC or FM so VCO 1 holds static pitch while VCO 2 gets sequenced modulation. OFF: PITCH CV still appears on the patchbay, usable as a generic 8-step CV sequencer.

behaviors.name: Patchbay as CV sequencer
behaviors.trigger: SEQ PITCH MOD OFF · patch PITCH / VELOCITY / TRIGGER outputs
behaviors.description: With SEQ PITCH MOD OFF, DFAM doubles as a step-CV sequencer: PITCH out, VELOCITY out and TRIGGER out can modulate another module (e.g., VCA CV, decay time, external filter cutoff). Gives two independent bipolar/unipolar CV rows per step plus a clock.

behaviors.name: Clock VCO is the tempo
behaviors.trigger: TEMPO knob or TEMPO CV jack
behaviors.description: The sequencer clock is itself a voltage-controlled oscillator (1V/oct on TEMPO input). This enables audio-rate clocking — the sequencer becomes an 8-step waveform generator. PITCH out then becomes an oscillator with per-sample amplitudes set by the 8 pitch knobs.

behaviors.name: External clock override
behaviors.description: TEMPO knob is ignored whenever ADV/CLOCK receives a rising edge. Use Mother-32 ASSIGN (Clock) or another DFAM's TRIGGER out to slave DFAM. Slowly-changing voltages without sharp edges may not trigger — accents from Mother-32 need fast rise to register.

behaviors.name: Hard-sync voice character
behaviors.description: VCO 2 phase locks to VCO 1. VCO 2 FREQUENCY becomes a timbre control rather than a pitch control — useful for sweeping metallic harmonics while bass pitch stays fixed. VCO 2 goes silent if set below VCO 1 frequency, especially on square wave.

behaviors.name: Filter self-oscillation
behaviors.description: In LP mode the VCF self-oscillates into a sine tone, usable as a third sound source (see 'Filter as Oscillator' preset). In HP mode the resonance scales differently — self-oscillation begins around CUTOFF ~1kHz with RESONANCE at max.

behaviors.name: Dual-function NOISE/VCF MOD
behaviors.description: NOISE/VCF MOD knob controls how much noise modulates cutoff (dirt/lo-fi). Patching VCF MOD replaces noise as the modulation source, and the knob now attenuates that external CV — so the knob must be CW for full CV depth.

behaviors.name: Sync multiple DFAMs
behaviors.description: Primary DFAM TRIGGER out → secondary DFAM ADV/CLOCK input. Start the secondary first (so it is ready), then start the primary. Chain more DFAMs the same way. Drives polyrhythmic patterns when primary tempo is varied independently.

behaviors.name: Eurorack installation
behaviors.description: Front panel unscrews from the wooden enclosure. Internal 10-pin header accepts standard Eurorack power ribbon. Uses +12V only (230mA max), no −12V. No power switch — unit is on when powered.

patchIdeas.name: DFAM + Mother-32 sync
patchIdeas.patch: Mother-32 ASSIGN out clocks DFAM's step advance so both instruments play locked together.
steps: On Mother-32, set the ASSIGN output to Clock (see Mother-32 manual Setup).
Patch Mother-32 ASSIGN out → DFAM ADV/CLOCK input.
Press RUN/STOP on DFAM first so it arms and waits for clock pulses.
Press RUN/STOP on Mother-32 — DFAM advances one step per incoming pulse.
Optional: patch Mother-32 KB out → DFAM VCO 1 CV for shared pitch tracking.
Patch DFAM AUDIO OUT (rear) → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: AUDIO OUT (rear 1/4") or VCA patch jack — DFAM drum voice clocked in lockstep with Mother-32.
patchIdeas.hear: DFAM fires one hit per Mother-32 clock pulse. Change Mother's internal clock division and DFAM's 8 steps shift against Mother's sequence polyrhythmically.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* RUN/STOP armed

Mother-32:
* ASSIGN: Clock

- Mother-32 (ASSIGN) c> DFAM (ADV/CLOCK)
- Mother-32 (KB) p> DFAM (VCO 1 CV) // optional tonal follow

patchIdeas.name: Dual DFAM chain
patchIdeas.patch: Primary DFAM's TRIGGER output clocks a secondary DFAM so both units step together with independent pitch/velocity/decay per step.
steps: Patch DFAM A (primary) TRIGGER out → DFAM B (secondary) ADV/CLOCK input.
On both DFAMs, set SEQ PITCH MOD switch to VCO 1&2 and dial contrasting PITCH and VELOCITY patterns.
Press RUN/STOP on DFAM B first so it is armed and waiting.
Press RUN/STOP on DFAM A — both units now step together.
Patch DFAM A VCA out → mixer left input.
Patch DFAM B VCA out → mixer right input for stereo layering.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Two VCA outputs — one drum voice per DFAM, panned hard L/R.
patchIdeas.hear: Both units advance on the same clock, but each has its own 8 pitches, velocities and decays — two layered drum voices perfectly locked in time.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM A:
* Primary · RUN/STOP armed second

DFAM B:
* Secondary · RUN/STOP armed first

- DFAM A (TRIGGER) c> DFAM B (ADV/CLOCK)
- DFAM A (VCA) -> Output (L)
- DFAM B (VCA) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Kick + snare in one voice
patchIdeas.patch: Alternate low/high PITCH per step and add noise on snare steps for a two-part drum pattern from a single DFAM.
steps: Start from default settings; press RUN/STOP to begin playback.
Set the SEQ PITCH MOD switch to VCO 1&2.
Turn PITCH knobs 1, 3, 5, 7 fully CCW (low = kick).
Turn PITCH knobs 2, 4, 6, 8 to around 1 o'clock (higher = snare).
Set VCO 1 EG AMOUNT to ~2 o'clock and VCO DECAY to ~9 o'clock for kick thump.
Set the VCA EG switch to FAST; set VCA DECAY to ~10 o'clock.
Turn NOISE/EXT LEVEL to ~1 o'clock so snare steps get body.
Optional: patch an external LFO → DFAM NOISE LEVEL input to swell noise across bars.
Patch DFAM VCA out → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — alternating kick+snare pattern, mono line-level.
patchIdeas.hear: Odd steps punch a deep pitched kick; even steps hit a brighter snare with noise body. At ~120 BPM this reads as a basic 4-on-the-floor beat.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* SEQ PITCH MOD: VCO 1&2
* PITCH 1,3,5,7: low (kick) · 2,4,6,8: mid (snare)
* VCO 1 EG AMT: 2 o'clock
* VCA EG: FAST · VCA DECAY: ~10 o'clock

- LFO (Out) >> DFAM (NOISE LEVEL) // optional noise swell

patchIdeas.name: Sequenced bass voice
patchIdeas.patch: DFAM plays a tuned 8-note bass line with long VCA decay and slow attack for glide-feeling notes.
steps: Set both VCO FREQUENCY knobs to noon and both VCO EG AMOUNT knobs to noon.
Set VCO 1 WAVE switch to triangle and turn VCO 2 LEVEL fully down.
Turn VCO 1 LEVEL to ~2 o'clock.
Set VCF switch to LP; CUTOFF to ~11 o'clock; RESONANCE to ~10 o'clock.
Set VCF EG AMOUNT to ~2 o'clock and VCF DECAY to ~11 o'clock for per-note filter bloom.
Set VCA EG switch to SLOW (100 ms attack emulates glide); VCA DECAY to ~12 o'clock.
Set SEQ PITCH MOD switch to VCO 1&2; tune PITCH 1-8 knobs by ear to shape the bass line.
Press RUN/STOP and refine each PITCH knob while it plays.
Patch DFAM VCA out → external VCA/mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — monophonic 8-step bass line, mono line-level.
patchIdeas.hear: Rubbery, laid-back bass line with filter bloom on each note. PITCH knobs are your note values; ADVANCE button auditions the next step while stopped.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* VCO 1 WAVE: triangle · VCO 2 LEVEL: 0
* VCA EG: SLOW · VCA DECAY: ~12 o'clock
* VCF: LP · VCF EG AMT: ~2 o'clock
* SEQ PITCH MOD: VCO 1&2

- Clock (Out) c> DFAM (ADV/CLOCK) // optional external clock

patchIdeas.name: Filter as percussive oscillator
patchIdeas.patch: Silence the VCOs and noise, push RESONANCE into self-oscillation, and let the VCF ping per step as a tuned sine drum.
steps: Turn VCO 1 LEVEL, VCO 2 LEVEL, and NOISE/EXT LEVEL fully CCW (all off).
Set VCF switch to LP.
Turn RESONANCE past 3 o'clock until a steady sine tone rings.
Set CUTOFF to ~11 o'clock and VCF EG AMOUNT to ~3 o'clock for ping depth.
Set VCF DECAY to ~9 o'clock so each hit is short and tuned.
Set SEQ PITCH MOD switch to OFF.
Patch DFAM PITCH out → DFAM VCF MOD input.
Turn NOISE/VCF MOD knob fully CW (full depth from the patched VCF MOD jack).
Dial PITCH 1-8 knobs to pitch each step of the tom sequence.
Press RUN/STOP; patch VCA out → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — pitched sine ping per step, tuned by the PITCH 1-8 knobs.
patchIdeas.hear: A melodic tom sequence made entirely by the self-oscillating ladder filter — no oscillators needed. Each PITCH knob sets the cutoff for that step, so the sine pings at a different note.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* VCO 1/2 LEVEL: 0 · NOISE/EXT LEVEL: 0
* VCF: LP · RESONANCE: ~3.5 o'clock
* CUTOFF: ~11 o'clock · VCF EG AMT: 3 o'clock · VCF DECAY: short
* SEQ PITCH MOD: OFF · NOISE/VCF MOD: full CW

- DFAM (PITCH) >> DFAM (VCF MOD) // sequenced cutoff tuning

patchIdeas.name: DFAM as 8-step CV sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: With SEQ PITCH MOD OFF, the PITCH and VELOCITY outputs become two independent step-CV rows driving external modules.
steps: Set SEQ PITCH MOD switch to OFF — the VCOs stop tracking the sequencer, but PITCH/VELOCITY/TRIGGER jacks remain live.
Patch DFAM PITCH out → destination 1 (e.g. external filter cutoff CV).
Patch DFAM VELOCITY out → destination 2 (e.g. external VCA CV).
Patch DFAM TRIGGER out → destination envelope gate/trigger input.
Press RUN/STOP on DFAM.
Tune PITCH 1-8 knobs for CV row 1 and VELOCITY 1-8 knobs for CV row 2 independently.
patchIdeas.signalOut: PITCH out (-5V to +5V), VELOCITY out (0 to +5V), TRIGGER out (~1ms +5V pulse) — three synced CV/trigger rows for any eurorack voice.
patchIdeas.hear: DFAM still plays internally, but its patchbay also sequences your external voice in lockstep — two independent step-CV rows plus a clock.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* SEQ PITCH MOD: OFF

- DFAM (PITCH) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- DFAM (VELOCITY) >> VCA (CV)
- DFAM (TRIGGER) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: FM Toys metallic hits
patchIdeas.patch: Linear FM from VCO 1 into VCO 2, with the sequencer driving only VCO 2, gives bell-like harmonics that shift per step.
steps: Set HARD SYNC switch to OFF (FM is most audible with sync off).
Turn 1→2 FM AMOUNT to ~2 o'clock.
Set VCO 1 WAVE to triangle and VCO 2 WAVE to square.
Turn VCO 1 LEVEL and VCO 2 LEVEL each to ~1 o'clock.
Set SEQ PITCH MOD switch to VCO 2 so only VCO 2 tracks the sequencer.
Tune PITCH 1-8 knobs for a sequence of metallic harmonics.
Optional: patch DFAM VCO 2 out → DFAM VCF MOD for audio-rate filter FM (see preset FM Toys, manual p. 34).
Press RUN/STOP; tweak VCO 2 FREQUENCY to sweep timbre without changing the bass note.
Patch VCA out → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — percussive bell/clang hits whose timbre shifts per step.
patchIdeas.hear: VCO 1 holds a steady fundamental; VCO 2 jumps around above it with FM sidebands, giving DX-style bells and aggressive metallic clangs that change per step.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* HARD SYNC: OFF
* 1→2 FM AMOUNT: 2 o'clock
* SEQ PITCH MOD: VCO 2

- DFAM (VCO 2) >> DFAM (VCF MOD) // filter FM at audio rate

patchIdeas.name: Sequencer as audio oscillator
patchIdeas.patch: Feed an audio-rate VCO into TEMPO input so the sequencer runs at audio rate and PITCH out becomes an 8-sample wavetable.
steps: Turn VCO 1 LEVEL, VCO 2 LEVEL, and NOISE/EXT LEVEL to 0 (silence the internal voice).
Set SEQ PITCH MOD switch to OFF.
Patch an external VCO (saw/triangle, audio rate) → DFAM TEMPO input.
Press RUN/STOP on DFAM — the sequencer is now clocking at audio rate.
Patch DFAM PITCH out → external filter or VCA input (this is your audio signal now).
Turn DFAM TEMPO knob to shift the fundamental (summed with the incoming CV).
Dial PITCH 1-8 knobs — each knob sets one sample of the 8-step waveform.
patchIdeas.signalOut: DFAM PITCH out — audio-rate stepped waveform (-5V to +5V), send through a VCA/filter for proper levels.
patchIdeas.hear: The sequencer becomes an oscillator. PITCH 1-8 are the 8 sample values of a wavetable; tweaking a knob changes one slice of the timbre without changing pitch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* VCO 1/2 LEVEL: 0 · Noise: 0 · SEQ PITCH MOD: OFF

- VCO (Out) -> DFAM (TEMPO) // audio-rate clock
- DFAM (PITCH) -> Filter (In) // 8-step wavetable out

patchIdeas.name: Velocity-driven accent from Mother-32
patchIdeas.patch: Mother-32 ASSIGN configured to MIDI Velocity drives DFAM's VELOCITY input so MIDI dynamics scale all three envelopes.
steps: On Mother-32, set the ASSIGN output to MIDI Velocity (see Mother-32 manual Setup).
Patch Mother-32 ASSIGN out → DFAM VELOCITY input — this overrides the sequencer VELOCITY 1-8 knobs.
Provide DFAM's clock from a second source (e.g. Mother-32 GATE out or another clock) → DFAM ADV/CLOCK.
Press RUN/STOP on DFAM.
Play Mother-32 via MIDI with varying velocities.
Patch DFAM VCA out → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — DFAM voice whose amplitude and decay scale with incoming MIDI velocity voltage.
patchIdeas.hear: Soft MIDI notes make DFAM hits quiet and short; hard notes punch loud with longer tails. All three envelopes (VCO/VCF/VCA) scale together.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Mother-32:
* ASSIGN: MIDI Velocity

DFAM:
* Sequencer running

- Mother-32 (GATE) c> DFAM (ADV/CLOCK)
- Mother-32 (ASSIGN) >> DFAM (VELOCITY) // velocity-scaled hits

patchIdeas.name: Quick hats — decay-modulated
patchIdeas.patch: Noise-only voice through the HP filter, with PITCH out modulating VCA DECAY so each step has its own tail length.
steps: Turn VCO 1 LEVEL and VCO 2 LEVEL to 0; turn NOISE/EXT LEVEL fully CW.
Set VCF switch to HP; CUTOFF to ~3 o'clock; RESONANCE to ~10 o'clock.
Set VCA EG switch to FAST; set VCA DECAY to noon (widest CV response per manual).
Set SEQ PITCH MOD switch to OFF so PITCH 1-8 does not change the VCOs.
Patch DFAM PITCH out → DFAM VCA DECAY input.
Tune PITCH 1-8 knobs — low knob = short closed hat, high knob = long open hat.
Press RUN/STOP.
Patch VCA out → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — filtered noise hats whose decay length changes per step.
patchIdeas.hear: A classic hi-hat line where PITCH 1-8 knobs control tightness rather than pitch. Two knobs high and the rest low gives you closed-closed-open-closed patterns.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* SEQ PITCH MOD: OFF
* VCO 1/2 LEVEL: 0 · NOISE: full
* VCF: HP · CUTOFF: ~3 o'clock
* VCA DECAY: noon · VCA EG: FAST

- DFAM (PITCH) >> DFAM (VCA DECAY) // decay varies per step

patchIdeas.name: Talking drum
patchIdeas.patch: VCO 2 audio-rate FMs the filter via VCF MOD, producing formant-like vocal timbres on each hit.
steps: Set HARD SYNC switch to OFF; turn 1→2 FM AMOUNT fully CCW.
Turn NOISE/EXT LEVEL to 0; turn NOISE/VCF MOD knob fully CW.
Set VCF switch to LP; CUTOFF to ~12 o'clock; RESONANCE low (~9 o'clock) for thump.
Set SEQ PITCH MOD switch to VCO 2 — sequencer changes VCO 2's frequency only.
Set VCO 1 LEVEL to ~1 o'clock (the fundamental); VCO 2 LEVEL to 0 (used only as mod source).
Patch DFAM VCO 2 out → DFAM VCF MOD input.
Press RUN/STOP and tune PITCH 1-8 knobs for different vowel shapes.
Patch VCA out → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — low drum tone shaped by a vocal-like filter formant each hit.
patchIdeas.hear: Each step hits a low drum, but the filter shouts a different vowel — 'bow-wow-wah' textures driven by VCO 2's sequenced pitch modulating cutoff at audio rate.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* HARD SYNC: OFF
* NOISE/EXT LEVEL: 0 · NOISE/VCF MOD: full CW
* VCF: LP · RESONANCE: low
* SEQ PITCH MOD: VCO 2

- DFAM (VCO 2) >> DFAM (VCF MOD)

patchIdeas.name: External source through DFAM VCA
patchIdeas.patch: External audio replaces noise at the mixer, and DFAM's sequencer chops it rhythmically via the VCA envelope.
steps: Patch external audio (synth pad, drum loop, reverb tail) → DFAM EXT AUDIO input. Signal must be ~10V p-p (eurorack level).
Turn VCO 1 LEVEL and VCO 2 LEVEL to 0.
Turn NOISE/EXT LEVEL fully CW for maximum external-signal gain.
Set VCF switch to LP; turn CUTOFF fully CW (filter wide open).
Set VCA EG switch to FAST; set VCA DECAY to taste (short for chops, long for swells).
Tune VELOCITY 1-8 knobs for per-step gain (0 = silent step).
Press RUN/STOP.
Patch DFAM VCA out → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — external source gated by DFAM's 8-step VCA envelope, per-step volume set by VELOCITY knobs.
patchIdeas.hear: The external track is sliced into 8 rhythmic chunks at DFAM's tempo. VELOCITY knobs set how loud each slice is; silent steps mute the source entirely.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DFAM:
* VCO 1/2 LEVEL: 0
* NOISE/EXT LEVEL: full
* VCF: LP · CUTOFF: full CW
* VCA EG: FAST · VCA DECAY: tuned to tempo

- External FX (Out) -> DFAM (EXT AUDIO)
- DFAM (VCA) -> Output (In) // rhythmically gated external signal
